title: Anvil Workbench plugin preview v1
description: The typed, redacted preview the plugin host returns before an effect-capable plugin request is authorized. It echoes the previewed request's `request_digest`, enumerates the bounded changes the effect would make, and carries the hash-bound approval binding for a gated action. An `install`, `upgrade`, or `downgrade` preview always declares approval required with a `payload_hash` that binds the exact request subject (R003 floor). Every object is closed and every human-readable field is a bounded, non-leaking safe string: no local path, raw command, URL/endpoint, credential, or unredacted payload is representable. It grants nothing on its own — it is the reviewable artifact an owner inspects before consuming a one-time approval. All prose is untrusted display data.

safeText.description: A bounded, human-safe string. The negative lookahead refuses a URL, host path, backslash, or credential shape so a preview line can never carry a raw endpoint, path, or token into the UI.

changes.description: The bounded, typed set of changes the effect would make, so an owner reviews a precise diff rather than a prose claim.

approval.description: The hash-bound approval binding. When required, `payload_hash` binds the exact typed subject of the previewed request and `action` names the catalog approval action. workbench.contracts.validate_plugin_preview enforces the R003 floor: an install/upgrade/downgrade preview must declare approval required with a bound payload hash.
